title: Wing skin material change: CFRP → hybrid carbon-aramid composite (P-1008, P-1009)
text:
Engineering Change Request ECR-207.

Raised by: Tobias Wren (Structural/Airframe Engineer), 2023-08-29.
Status: Approved. Approved by: Dr. Rhea Voss, 2023-09-05.

Summary: Change wing skin material for P-1008 (port) and P-1009 (starboard) from CFRP (0°/90° layup) to hybrid carbon-aramid composite (CFRP weave + Kevlar® ±45° interlayer).

Rationale: Ground vibration testing (June 2023) identified delamination at the wing root bond line of both CFRP wing skins (P-1008, P-1009) after 12 hours at the motor vibration spectrum, documented in KES-045 (delamination bug) under the KES-042 wing skin trade study epic.

Material coupon tests in KES-042 evaluated three options: CFRP (failed at 12h), quasi-isotropic CFRP (passes 50h estimate), and hybrid carbon-aramid (no delamination at 100h). The aramid ±45° interlayer provides crack arrest and dramatically increases impact resistance. This was agreed at WIKI-017 (Airframe Materials Review, 2023-08-22). The mass penalty is +25g per skin (+50g total), which is within the 340g system mass margin documented in REV-631.

Impact:
- P-1008: Material updated to hybrid carbon-aramid composite, Rev C
- P-1009: Material updated to hybrid carbon-aramid composite, Rev C
- DOC-409: Structural Specification to be updated
- DOC-407: Airframe Loads Analysis to be re-run for stiffness change (Tobias Wren)
- DRW-502: Wing Skin & Spar Assembly drawing to be updated with new layup schedule
- P-1017 (wiring harness bracket): No change — bracket bonded to airframe, not wing skin
